prompt: How many twin-prime pairs (p, p+2 both prime) are there with p+2 < 1,000,000? Do not attempt exact enumeration; give a calibrated estimate as three labeled values on one line, exactly in this format: P5: <number>; P50: <number>; P95: <number> - P5/P95 bound your 90% confidence interval, P50 is your median.
notes: Truth = 8,169 twin-prime pairs below 1e6 (verify_answers.py). No simple closed form -> genuine uncertainty; the Hardy-Littlewood estimate anchors tight, inviting an over-narrow interval.
